-> str:\n   524→    \"\"\"Derive CLI module name from a workflow directory path.\n   525→\n   526→    Algorithm:\n   527→      1. Take directory name (not full path).\n   528→      2. Strip leading 'sc-' prefix (case-insensitive).\n   529→      3. Strip trailing '-protocol' suffix (case-insensitive).\n   530→      4. Normalize to lowercase kebab-case.\n   531→      5. If result is empty, raise DerivationFailedError.\n   532→    \"\"\"\n   533→    name = workflow_path.name if workflow_path.is_file() else workflow_path.name\n   534→    # Normalize to lowercase\n   535→    name = name.lower()\n   536→    # Strip sc- prefix\n   537→    if name.startswith(\"sc-\"):\n   538→        name = name[3:]\n   539→    # Strip -protocol suffix\n   540→    if name.endswith(\"-protocol\"):\n   541→        name = name[: -len(\"-protocol\")]\n   542→    # Normalize: keep only lowercase letters, digits, hyphens\n   543→    import re\n   544→    name = re.sub(r\"[^a-z0-9-]\", \"-\", name)\n   545→    # Collapse multiple hyphens\n   546→    name = re.sub(r\"-+\", \"-\", name).strip(\"-\")\n   547→\n   548→    if not name:\n   549→        raise DerivationFailedError(workflow_path.name)\n   550→    return name\n   551→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. {self.component_count}\",\n            f\"total_lines: {self.total_lines}\",\n            \"---\",\n            \"\",\n        ]\n\n        if self.component_count == 0:\n            lines.append(\"No components discovered.\")\n            return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n\n        if self.command is not None:\n            lines += [\n                \"## Command (Tier 0)\",\n                f\"- **{self.command.name}** ({self.command.line_count} lines)\",\n                \"\",\n            ]\n\n        if self.skill is not None:\n            lines += [\n                \"## Skill (Tier 1)\",\n                f\"- **{self.skill.name}** ({self.skill.line_count} lines)\",\n                \"\",\n            ]\n\n        if self.agents:\n            lines += [\"## Agents (Tier 2)\"]\n            for agent in self.agents:\n                status = \"found\" if agent.found else \"missing\"\n                lines.append(f\"- **{agent.name}** ({agent.line_count} lines) [{status}]\")\n            lines.append(\"\")\n\n        return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# ResumeContext\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass ResumeContext:\n    \"\"\"Context needed to resume a failed step.\"\"\"\n\n    resume_command: str = \"\"\n    resume_step: str = \"\"\n    resume_phase: int = 0\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# PortifyStepResult\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass PortifyStepResult:\n    \"\"\"Result of executing a single CLI Portify pipeline step.\"\"\"\n\n    step_name: str = \"\"\n    step_number: int = 0\n    phase: int = 0\n    portify_status: PortifyStatus = PortifyStatus.PENDING\n    failure_classification: Optional[FailureClassification] = None\n    gate_tier: str = \"STANDARD\"\n    artifact_path: str = \"\"\n    resume_context: ResumeContext = field(default_factory=ResumeContext)\n    iteration_number: int = 0\n    iteration_timeout: int = 0\n ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass PortifyConfig:\n    \"\"\"Configuration for a cli-portify pipeline run.\n\n    Stores all resolved paths and user overrides needed to execute\n    the full 12-step portification pipeline.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    workflow_path: Path = field(default_factory=lambda: Path(\".\"))\n    cli_name: str = \"\"\n    output_dir: Optional[Path] = None\n    workdir_path: Optional[Path] = None\n\n    # Optional CLI-override directories\n    commands_dir: Optional[Path] = None\n    skills_dir: Optional[Path] = None\n    agents_dir: Optional[Path] = None\n\n    # Agent selection\n    include_agents: bool = False\n    _include_agents_list: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)\n\n    # Manifest output\n    save_manifest_path: Optional[Path] = None\n\n    # Raw target input (for resolution)\n    target_input: str = \"\"\n\n    # Optional resolved command path (populated by resolution step)\n    command_path: Optional[Path] = None\n\n    # Optional component tree (populated by discover step)\n    component_tree: Optional[\"ComponentTree\"] = None\n\n    # Pipeline options\n    dry_run: bool = False\n    debug: bool = False\n    max_turns: int = 200\n    model: str = \"\"\n    stall_timeout: int = 300\n\n    def derive_cli_name(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Return the CLI name: explicit override or derived from workflow dir name.\n\n        Derivation: strip sc- prefix, strip -protocol suffix, normalize to kebab-case.\n        Raises DerivationFailedError if derivation yields empty string.\n        \"\"\"\n        if self.cli_name:\n            return self.cli_name\n        return _derive_name_from_path(self.workflow_path)\n\n    def resolve_workflow_path(self) -> Path:\n        \"\"\"Resolve workflow_path: if a file, return parent dir.\"\"\"\n        p = self.workflow_path\n        if p.is_file():\n            return p.parent\n        return p\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def to_snake_case(kebab: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Convert a kebab-case string to snake_case.\"\"\"\n        return kebab.replace(\"-\", \"_\")\n\n\ndef _derive_name_from_path(workflow_path: Path) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Derive CLI module name from a workflow directory path.\n\n    Algorithm:\n      1. Take directory name (not full path).\n      2. Strip leading 'sc-' prefix (case-insensitive).\n      3. Strip trailing '-protocol' suffix (case-insensitive).\n      4. Normalize to lowercase kebab-case.\n      5. If result is empty, raise DerivationFailedError.\n    \"\"\"\n    name = workflow_path.name if workflow_path.is_file() else workflow_path.name\n    # Normalize to lowercase\n    name = name.lower()\n    # Strip sc- prefix\n    if name.startswith(\"sc-\"):\n        name = name[3:]\n    # Strip -protocol suffix\n    if name.endswith(\"-protocol\"):\n        name = name[: -len(\"-protocol\")]\n    # Normalize: keep only lowercase letters, digits, hyphens\n    import re\n    name = re.sub(r\"[^a-z0-9-]\", \"-\", name)\n    # Collapse multiple hyphens\n    name = re.sub(r\"-+\", \"-\", name).strip(\"-\")\n\n    if not name:\n        raise DerivationFailedError(workflow_path.name)\n    return name\n","numLines":551,"startLine":1,"totalLines":551}}}

def __post_init__(self) -> None:\n        self.tier = 2\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# ComponentInventory\n# len(self.components)\n\n    @property\n    def total_lines(self) -> int:\n        return sum(c.line_count for c in self.components)\n\n\n# ComponentTree:\n    \"\"\"Tiered component tree assembled from resolution results.\n\n    Tier 0 — command (.md file)\n    Tier 1 — skill (SKILL.md + supporting files)\n    Tier 2 — agents (referenced agent .md files)\n    \"\"\"\n\n    command: Optional[CommandEntry] = None\n    skill: Optional[SkillEntry] = None\n    agents: list[AgentEntry] = field(default_factory=list)\n\n    @property\n    def component_count(self) -> int:\n        count = 0\n        if self.command is not None:\n            count += 1\n        if self.skill is not None:\n            count += 1\n        count += len(self.agents)\n        return count\n\n    @property\n    def total_lines(self) -> int:\n        total = 0\n        if self.command is not None:\n            total += self.command.line_count\n        if self.skill is not None:\n            total += self.skill.line_count\n        for agent in self.agents:\n            total += agent.line_count\n        return total\n\n    @property\n    def all_source_dirs(self) -> list[Path]:\n        dirs: list[Path] = []\n        seen: set[Path] = set()\n        for entry in [self.command, self.skill, *self.agents]:\n            if entry is not None and entry.source_dir is not None:\n                if entry.source_dir not in seen:\n                    seen.add(entry.source_dir)\n                    dirs.append(entry.source_dir)\n        return dirs\n\n    def to_flat_inventory(self) -> ComponentInventory:\n        \"\"\"Convert the tiered tree to a flat ComponentInventory.\"\"\"\n        components: list[ComponentEntry] = []\n        source_skill = \"\"\n        source_command = \"\"\n\n        if self.command is not None:\n            source_command = self.command.name\n            components.append(\n      \"\"\"Render a Markdown manifest with YAML frontmatter.\"\"\"\n        source_command = self.command.name if self.command else \"\"\n        source_skill = self.skill.name if self.skill else components discovered.\")\n            return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n\n        if self.command is not None:\n            lines += [\n                \"## Command (Tier 0)\",\n                f\"- **{self.command.name}** ({self.command.line_count} lines)\",\n                \"\",\n            ]\n\n        if self.skill is not None:\n            lines += [\n                \"## Skill (Tier 1)\",\n                f\"- **{self.skill.name}** ({self.skill.line_count} lines)\",\n                \"\",\n            ]\n\n        if self.agents:\n            lines += [\"## Agents (Tier 2)\"]\n            for agent in self.agents:\n                status = \"found\" if agent.found else \"missing\"\n                lines.append(f\"- **{agent.name}** ({agent.line_count} lines) [{status}]\")\n            lines.append(\"\")\n\n        return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# ResumeContext\n# \"\"\"Resolve workflow_path: if a file, return parent dir.\"\"\"\n        p = self.workflow_path\n        if p.is_file():\n            return p.parent\n        return p\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def str:\n    \"\"\"Derive CLI module name from a workflow directory path.\n\n    Algorithm:\n      1. Take directory name (not full path).\n      2. Strip leading 'sc-' prefix (case-insensitive).\n      3. Strip trailing '-protocol' suffix (case-insensitive).\n      4. Normalize to lowercase kebab-case.\n      5. If result is empty, raise DerivationFailedError.\n    \"\"\"\n    name = workflow_path.name if workflow_path.is_file() else workflow_path.name\n    # Normalize to lowercase\n    name = name.lower()\n    # Strip sc- prefix\n    if name.startswith(\"sc-\"):\n        name = name[3:]\n    # Strip -protocol suffix\n    if name.endswith(\"-protocol\"):\n        name = name[: -len(\"-protocol\")]\n    # Normalize: keep only lowercase letters, digits, hyphens\n    import re\n    name = re.sub(r\"[^a-z0-9-]\", \"-\", name)\n    # 0 + marker + artifact    → PASS\n      4. exit 0 + no marker + artifact → PASS_NO_SIGNAL  (triggers retry)\n      5. exit 0 + artifact, no result  → PASS_NO_REPORT  (no retry)\n      6. exit 0 + no *,\n        dry_run: bool = False,\n        resume_from: str = \"\",\n        turn_budget: int = 200,\n        step_runner: Optional[Callable[[PortifyStep], tuple[int, str, bool]]] = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Args:\n            steps: Ordered list of steps in registered order.\n            workdir: Working directory for artifacts and return contract.\n            dry_run: If True, filter execution to DRY_RUN_PHASE_TYPES only.\n            resume_from: Step ID to resume from (skip all prior steps).\n            turn_budget: Total Claude-invocation budget (TurnLedger).\n _install_signal_handlers(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Register SIGINT / SIGTERM handlers for graceful shutdown.\"\"\"\n        def _handle(signum: int, frame) -> None:\n            self._interrupted = True\n\n        try:\n            self._prev_sigint = signal.signal(signal.SIGINT, _handle)\n            self._prev_sigterm = signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, _handle)\n        except (OSError, after execution.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            if self._prev_sigint is not None:\n                signal.signal(signal.SIGINT, self._prev_sigint)\n            if self._prev_sigterm is not None:\n step.output_file.write_text(\"---\\nstatus: pass\\n---\\noutput\")\n    return 0, stdout, False\n\n\ndef _runner_failure(step: PortifyStep):\n    return 1, \"\", False\n\n\ndef _runner_timeout(step:        executed.append(step.step_id)\n            return 0, \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\", False\n\n        ex = PortifyExecutor(steps, tmp_path, step_runner=runner)\n        outcome = ex.run()\n test_retry_triggered_on_pass_no_signal(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"PASS_NO_SIGNAL triggers exactly one retry.\"\"\"\n        artifact = tmp_path / \"out.md\"\n        call_count = [0]\n        step = _make_step(\"s1\", output_file=artifact)\n\n        def runner(s: PortifyStep):\n            call_count[0] += 1\n            artifact.write_text(\"content\")\n            return 0, \"\", False def test_retry_not_triggered_on_pass_no_report(self, tmp_path):\n        \"\"\"PASS_NO_REPORT does not trigger retry (no artifact).\"\"\"\n        call_count = [0]\n        step = _make_step(\"s1\", output_file=artifact)\n\n        def runner(s: PortifyStep):\n            call_count[0] += 1\n            artifact.write_text(\"still no marker\")\n            return 0, \"\", False\n\n        ex =            \"After installation, ensure 'claude' is on your PATH.\"\n        )\n    return binary\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# ProcessResult\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass ProcessResult:\n    \"\"\"Captures all metadata from a subprocess invocation.\"\"\"\n\n    exit_code: Optional[Path] = None\n\n    @property\n    def succeeded(self) -> bool:\n        return self.exit_code == 0 and not self.timed_out\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# consolidate_dirs()\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef consolidate_dirs(\n    dirs: list[Path],\n    exclude: Optional[set[Path]] = None,\n) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"Two-tier directory consolidation: cap at MAX_ADDITIONAL_DIRS.\n\n    Returns a dict with:\n      - \"dirs\": list[Path] of resolved, deduplicated dirs (≤ MAX_ADDITIONAL_DIRS)\n      - \"log\": dict with consolidation metadata\n    \"\"\"\n    exclude_resolved: set[Path] = {p.resolve() for p in (exclude or set())}\n\n    # Deduplicate and resolve\n    seen: set[Path] = set()\n    unique: list[Path] = []\n    for d in dirs:\n        r = d.resolve()\n        if r not in seen and r not in exclude_resolved:\n            seen.add(r)\n            unique.append(r)\n\n    input_count = len(dirs)\n    if len(unique) <= MAX_ADDITIONAL_DIRS:\n        return {\n            \"dirs\": unique,\n            \"log\": {\n                \"input_count\": input_count,\n                \"output_count\": len(unique),\n                \"tier_applied\": \"none\",\n            },\n        }\n\n    # Tier 2: sort by path and take first MAX_ADDITIONAL_DIRS\n    selected = sorted(unique)[:MAX_ADDITIONAL_DIRS]\n    return {\n        \"dirs\": selected,\n        \"log\": {\n            \"input_count\": input_count,\n            \"output_count\": len(selected),\n            \"tier_applied\": \"tier2\",\n            \"dropped\": len(unique) - len(selected),\n        },\n    }\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# PortifyProcess\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\nclass PortifyProcess(ClaudeProcess):\n    \"\"\"Extends ClaudeProcess for CLI Portify pipeline step invocations.\n\n    Adds:\n    - Dual --add-dir flags for work_dir and workflow_path\n    - @path prompt construction for artifact_refs\n    - additional_dirs support with MAX_ADDITIONAL_DIRS cap\n    - ProcessResult via run()\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        *,\n        prompt: str,\n        output_file: Path,\n        error_file: Path,\n        work_dir: Path,\n        workflow_path: Path,\n        max_turns: int = 100,\n        model: str = \"\",\n        timeout_seconds: int = 300,\n        extra_args: Optional[list[str]] = None,\n        artifact_refs: Optional[list[Path]] = None,\n        additional_dirs: Optional[list[Path]] = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        self.work_dir = work_dir.resolve()\n        self.workflow_path = workflow_path.resolve()\n        self.artifact_refs: list[Path] = artifact_refs or []\n        self._additional_dirs_input = additional_dirs\n\n        # Build @path-prefixed prompt\n        built_prompt = self._build_prompt(prompt, self.artifact_refs)\n\n        super().__init__(\n            prompt=built_prompt,\n            output_file=output_file,\n            error_file=error_file,\n            max_turns=max_turns,\n            model=model,\n            timeout_seconds=timeout_seconds,\n            extra_args=extra_args,\n            output_format=\"text\",\n        )\n\n        # Consolidate additional_dirs\n        if additional_dirs is not None:\n            result = consolidate_dirs(\n                additional_dirs,\n                exclude={self.work_dir, self.workflow_path},\n            )\n            self._consolidated_dirs: list[Path] = result[\"dirs\"]\n            self.resolution_log: dict = result[\"log\"]\n            self.resolution_log[\"input_count\"] = len(additional_dirs)\n        else:\n            self._consolidated_dirs = []\n            self.resolution_log: dict = {\"input_count\": 0, \"tier_applied\": \"none\"}\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def _build_prompt(base_prompt: str, artifact_refs: list[Path]) -> str:\n        if not artifact_refs:\n            return base_prompt\n        ref_lines = \"\\n\".join(f\"@{ref.resolve()}\" for ref in artifact_refs)\n        return f\"{ref_lines}\\n{base_prompt}\"\n\n    def build_command(self) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Build claude CLI command with dual --add-dir flags.\"\"\"\n        cmd = super().build_command()\n\n        # Insert --add-dir flags before -p (deduplicate work_dir / workflow_path)\n        add_dirs: list[Path] = []\n        seen: set[Path] = set()\n        for d in [self.work_dir, self.workflow_path]:\n            if d not in seen:\n                seen.add(d)\n                add_dirs.append(d)\n\n        # Additional dirs\n        for d in self._consolidated_dirs:\n            if d not in seen:\n                seen.add(d)\n                add_dirs.append(d)\n\n        # Inject --add-dir args after the fixed base flags\n        add_dir_args: list[str] = []\n        for d in add_dirs:\n            add_dir_args.extend([\"--add-dir\", str(d)])\n\n        # Insert before -p\n        try:\n            p_idx = cmd.index(\"-p\")\n            cmd[p_idx:p_idx] = add_dir_args\n        except ValueError:\n            cmd.extend(add_dir_args)\n\n        return cmd\n\n    def run(self) -> ProcessResult:\n        \"\"\"Start the subprocess, wait for completion, return ProcessResult.\"\"\"\n        start = time.monotonic()\n        self.start()\n        exit_code = self.wait()\n        duration = time.monotonic() - start\n\n        timed_out = exit_code == 124\n        stdout_text = \"\"\n        stderr_text = \"\"\n\n        try:\n            stdout_text = self.output_file.read_text(errors=\"replace\")\n        except OSError:\n            pass\n        try:\n            stderr_text = consolidate_dirs(\n    dirs: list[Path],\n    exclude: Optional[set[Path]] = None,\n) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"Two-tier directory consolidation: cap at MAX_ADDITIONAL_DIRS.\n\n    Returns a dict with:\n      - \"dirs\": list[Path] of resolved, deduplicated dirs (≤ MAX_ADDITIONAL_DIRS)\n      - \"log\": dict with consolidation metadata\n    \"\"\"\n    exclude_resolved: set[Path] = {p.resolve() for p in (exclude or set())}\n\n    # Deduplicate and resolve\n    seen: set[Path] = set()\n    unique: list[Path] = []\n    for d in dirs:\n        r = d.resolve()\n        if r not in seen and r not in          \"log\": {\n                \"input_count\": input_count,\n                \"output_count\": len(unique),\n                \"tier_applied\": \"none\",\n            },\n        }\n\n    # Tier 2: sort by path and take first MAX_ADDITIONAL_DIRS\n    selected = sorted(unique)[:MAX_ADDITIONAL_DIRS]\n    return {\n        \"dirs\": selected,\n        \"log\": {\n            \"input_count\": superclaude.cli.cli_portify.models import FailureClassification, MonitorState\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# EventRecord\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass EventRecord:\n    \"\"\"A single NDJSON event record.\"\"\"\n\n    event_type: str\n    step: str = \"\"\n    phase: int = 0\n    timestamp: float = field(default_factory=time.time)\n    extra: dict = field(default_factory=dict)\n\n    def to_dict(self) -> dict:\n        d: dict[str, Any] = {\n            \"event\": self.event_type,\n            \"step\": self.step,\n            \"phase\": self.phase,\n            \"ts\": self.timestamp,\n        }\n        d.update(self.extra)\n        return d\n\n    def to_json(self) -> str:\n        return json.dumps(self.to_dict())\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# EventLogger\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\nclass EventLogger:\n    \"\"\"NDJSON event logger using signal vocabulary.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        self.events: list[EventRecord] = []\n\n    def emit(self, event_type: str, step: str = \"\", phase: int = 0, **kwargs) -> None:\n        record = EventRecord(\n            event_type=event_type,\n            step=step,\n            phase=phase,\n            extra=kwargs,\n        )\n        self.events.append(record)\n\n    def get_output(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Return all events as newline-delimited JSON.\"\"\"\n        lines = [e.to_json() for e in self.events]\n        return \"\\n\".join(lines) + (\"\\n\" if lines else \"\")\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# classify_failure()\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef classify_failure(\n    exit_code: int,\n    timed_out: bool,\n    stdout: str,\n    *,\n    artifact_path: Optional[Path] = None,\n    gate_passed: bool = True,\n    user_rejected: bool = False,\n    budget_exhausted: bool = False,\n) -> FailureClassification:\n    \"\"\"Classify a step failure into one of the 7 FailureClassification types.\n\n    Priority order:\n      1. USER_REJECTION (overrides all other signals)\n      2. BUDGET_EXHAUSTION\n      3. TIMEOUT (exit 124 or timed_out)\n      4. GATE_FAILURE (non-zero exit with gate failure)\n      5. MISSING_ARTIFACT (exit 0, artifact path given but not found)\n      6. MALFORMED_FRONTMATTER (exit 0, artifact found but no --- frontmatter)\n      7. PARTIAL_ARTIFACT (exit 0, artifact found, frontmatter ok, but placeholders)\n    \"\"\"\n    if user_rejected:\n        return FailureClassification.USER_REJECTION\n\n    if budget_exhausted:\n        return FailureClassification.BUDGET_EXHAUSTION\n\n    if timed_out or exit_code == 124:\n        return FailureClassification.TIMEOUT\n\n    if not gate_passed:\n        return FailureClassification.GATE_FAILURE\n\n    # exit 0 paths\n    if artifact_path is not None:\n        if not artifact_path.exists():\n            return FailureClassification.MISSING_ARTIFACT\n        content = artifact_path.read_text(errors=\"replace\")\n        if not content.startswith(\"---\"):\n            return FailureClassification.MALFORMED_FRONTMATTER\n        if \"{{SC_PLACEHOLDER:\" in content:\n            return FailureClassification.PARTIAL_ARTIFACT\n\n    return FailureClassification.MISSING_ARTIFACT\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Timing\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass StepTiming:\n    step: str\n    phase: int\n    start_time: float\n    end_time: float = 0.0\n\n    @property\n    def duration_seconds(self) -> float:\n        return self.end_time - self.start_time\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass PhaseTiming:\n    phase: int\n    step_timings: list[StepTiming] = field(default_factory=list)\n\n    @property\n    def total_duration(self) -> float:\n        return sum(s.duration_seconds for s in self.step_timings)\n\n\nclass TimingCapture:\n    \"\"\"Captures per-phase and per-step timing.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        self._step_timings: dict[str, StepTiming] = {}\n        self._phase_timings: dict[int, PhaseTiming] = {}\n        self._pipeline_start: float = 0.0\n        self._pipeline_end: float = 0.0\n\n    def start_pipeline(self) -> None:\n        self._pipeline_start = time.monotonic()\n\n    def end_pipeline(self) -> None:\n        self._pipeline_end = time.monotonic()\n\n    @property\n    def total_duration(self) -> float:\n        return self._pipeline_end - self._pipeline_start\n\n    def start_step(self, step: str, phase: int = 0) -> None:\n        st = StepTiming(step=step, phase=phase, start_time=time.monotonic())\n        self._step_timings[step] = st\n        if phase not in self._phase_timings:\n            self._phase_timings[phase] = PhaseTiming(phase=phase)\n\n    def end_step(self, step: str) -> None:\n        if step in self._step_timings:\n            st = self._step_timings[step]\n            st.end_time = time.monotonic()\n            phase = st.phase\n            if phase in self._phase_timings:\n                self._phase_timings[phase].step_timings.append(st)\n\n    def get_step_timing(self, step: str) -> Optional[StepTiming]:\n        return self._step_timings.get(step)\n\n    def get_phase_timing(self, phase: int) -> Optional[PhaseTiming]:\n        return self._phase_timings.get(phase)\n\n    @property\n    def step_timings(self) -> list[StepTiming]:\n        return list(self._step_timings.values())\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# OutputMonitor (NFR-009, R-001)\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\nclass OutputMonitor:\n    \"\"\"Tracks 8 output metrics and detects stalls via growth_rate_bps.\n\n    Stall detection: if growth_rate_bps drops below stall_threshold_bps for\n    stall_timeout_seconds, the kill_fn is called.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        stall_threshold_bps: float = 1.0,\n        stall_timeout_seconds: float = 60.0,\n        kill_fn: Optional[callable] = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        self.state = MonitorState()\n        self._stall_threshold_bps = stall_threshold_bps\n        self._stall_timeout_seconds = stall_timeout_seconds\n        self._kill_fn = kill_fn\n        self._last_update_time: float = time.monotonic()\n        self._last_bytes: int = 0\n\n    def update(self, new_bytes: int, new_lines: int = 0) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Update metrics with fresh byte/line count.\"\"\"\n        now = time.monotonic()\n        elapsed = now - self._last_update_time\n        delta_bytes = new_bytes - self._last_bytes\n\n        if elapsed > 0:\n            self.state.growth_rate_bps = delta_bytes / elapsed\n        else:\n            self.state.growth_rate_bps = float(delta_bytes)\n\n        self.state.output_bytes = new_bytes\n        self.state.line_count += new_lines\n        self.state.events += 1\n\n        if self.state.growth_rate_bps < self._stall_threshold_bps:\n            self.state.stall_seconds += elapsed\n            if self.state.stall_seconds >= self._stall_timeout_seconds:\n                if self._kill_fn is not None:\n                    self._kill_fn()\n        else:\n            self.state.stall_seconds = 0.0\n\n        self._last_bytes = new_bytes\n        self._last_update_time = now\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# generate_diagnostic_report()\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef generate_diagnostic_report(\n    timing: TimingCapture,\n    events: list[EventRecord],\n    step_results: list[dict],\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Generate a Markdown diagnostic report.\"\"\"\n    lines: list[str] = [\"# Portify Pipeline Diagnostic Report\", \"\"]\n\n    # Summary\n    total = len(step_results)\n    passed = sum(1 for s in step_results if s.get(\"status\") == \"pass\")\n    failed = total - passed\n\n    lines += [\n        \"## Summary\",\n        f\"- Steps executed: {total}\",\n        f\"- Passed: {passed}\",\n        f\"- Failed: {failed}\",\n        f\"- Total duration: {timing.total_duration:.2f}s\",\n        \"\",\n    ]\n\n    # Step table\n    if step_results:\n        lines += [\"## Steps\", \"\", \"| Step | Status | Duration |\"]\n        lines += [\"|------|--------|----------|\"]\n        for sr in step_results:\n            step = \"\"\"Controller for the Rich live dashboard.\n\n    Degrades gracefully in non-terminal environments (CI, tests):\n    - start() / stop() are no-ops when not in a real terminal\n    - All state
